game_name: Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back

danger_signals.visual_form: Multiple enemies appearing simultaneously in viewport
danger_signals.meaning: High threat state; player must evade or engage multiple hostiles

danger_signals.visual_form: Projectiles and melee strikes originating from beyond visible play area
danger_signals.meaning: Unpredictable danger; forces player awareness of screen boundaries

danger_signals.visual_form: Projectile streams in distinctive Star Wars aesthetic
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat engagement; deflectable by Luke with lightsaber or avoidable by all characters

danger_signals.visual_form: Large Mode 7 scaled mechanical structure dominating screen space
danger_signals.meaning: Boss-level environmental threat; iconic Empire military asset requiring special approach

danger_signals.visual_form: Character damage animation and health bar reduction
danger_signals.meaning: Cumulative danger; player approaching defeat state

safety_signals.signal: Absence of enemies in stage section
safety_signals.visual_form: Clear platforming environment without hostile units
safety_signals.meaning: Safe traversal zone; opportunity for exploration and navigation

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items: secondary weapons for Han/Chewbacca, Force Powers for Luke post-Dagobah
safety_signals.meaning: Capability enhancement; signals progression and preparation for upcoming threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Game allows mid-stage saves via password system
safety_signals.meaning: Reduced punishment for failure; lower frustration, player checkpoint security

safety_signals.visual_form: Luke's lightsaber block animation, Han's grenade throw, Chewbacca's spinning attack
safety_signals.meaning: Available mitigation options; tactical safety through character abilities

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Rebel soldier appearance in Ice Fields of Hoth cutscenes; playable characters Luke, Han, Chewbacca
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hoth base interiors, snow terrain, technological Rebel installations

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Darth Vader as final boss; Imperial military walkers (AT-AT), stormtroopers inferred from blaster-wielding enemies
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types representing Imperial forces across levels; blaster-equipped hostiles
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ice Fields of Hoth (contested Imperial target zone), industrial/mechanical level environments, imperial installations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Snow-covered Hoth terrain, ice fields dominating visual palette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Luke operates without Force Powers; characters encounter standard Imperial opposition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition to darker, more technologically-advanced environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Luke gains collectible Force Powers for use in later levels; enhanced capability signaling progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final confrontation arena; climactic visual presentation consistent with film source material
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss battle requires application of accumulated weapons, powers, and learned enemy patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple hostile units visible simultaneously in single screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased enemy spawn rate; elevated difficulty requiring precision and avoidance tactics

environmental_storytelling.technique: Cinematic displays faithful to film source
environmental_storytelling.example: Cutscenes featuring Rebel soldiers in Hoth Ice Fields with contest winner Jeff Crosno's likeness integrated into existing facial graphics; establishes narrative continuity with The Empire Strikes Back film

environmental_storytelling.technique: Mode 7 vehicle sequences as world-state markers
environmental_storytelling.example: AT-AT walker attack stage using Mode 7 scaling effects; visually demonstrates Empire's technological superiority and presence in player-controlled environment

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character-specific move animations as environmental communication
environmental_storytelling.example: Luke's lightsaber deflection animation signals Jedi capability; Han's grenade throw indicates Smuggler resourcefulness; Chewbacca's spinning attack demonstrates physical prowess—each conveys faction identity through gameplay aesthetics
Transition from Hoth snow fields to swamp planet Dagobah to darker industrial environments communicates Empire Strikes Back narrative arc; environment changes mirror story progression

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard platforming attacks; no Force Powers available; lightsaber basic strike behavior
Access to collectible Force Powers; lightsaber can block and deflect blaster fire; enhanced defensive and offensive capability
Primary and secondary weapons available; can throw grenades as tactical option; represents gunslinger archetype with ranged combat focus
Possesses spinning attack power-up; melee-focused character; demonstrates brute-force tactical approach distinct from Luke's Force-based and Han's ranged mechanics
Fire projectiles in player direction; can attack from off-screen; represent baseline Imperial threat requiring evasion or deflection
Complex boss-battle behavior requiring accumulated player skill, weapons, and Force Powers; represents narrative climax and ultimate faction threat
Large-scale Mode 7 presentation; mechanized movement pattern; requires specific tactical approach distinct from standard platforming; demonstrates Empire's military infrastructure visually

notes: Game communicates world-state primarily through visual theming and character progression mechanics rather than explicit UI indicators. The transition from Hoth to Dagobah to Imperial installations mirrors narrative progression. Enemy concentration and variety increase as difficulty escalates. Character abilities serve dual purpose: mechanical (gameplay tools) and diegetic (faction/identity signals). Luke's Force Powers collection post-Dagobah functions as clear world-state progression marker. AT-AT battle represents pinnacle of Empire military threat visualization. Lightsaber deflection mechanic visually communicates Luke's Jedi status and provides tangible defensive tool against Empire blaster fire.
